contents.value:
```verilog
module counter (
    input wire clk,
    input wire rst,
    output reg [7:0] count
);
```
**module** is the primary design unit in Verilog/PLA. Contains ports, internal logic, and behavioral descriptions.

```verilog
and g1(out, a, b);
and #5 g2(out, a, b, c);  // with delay
```
**and** gate primitive. Outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1. Optional delay specification.

```verilog
or g1(out, a, b);
or #3 g2(out, a, b, c, d);  // multi-input
```
**or** gate primitive. Outputs 1 when any input is 1.

```verilog
not g1(out, in);
not #2 (out1, in), (out2, in);  // multiple outputs
```
**not** (inverter) gate primitive. Outputs the logical complement of the input.

```verilog
nand g1(out, a, b);
```
**nand** gate primitive. Outputs 0 only when all inputs are 1. Universal gate — can implement any logic function.

```verilog
nor g1(out, a, b);
```
**nor** gate primitive. Outputs 1 only when all inputs are 0. Also a universal gate.

```verilog
xor g1(out, a, b);
assign parity = ^data;  // reduction XOR
```
**xor** gate primitive. Outputs 1 when inputs differ. Reduction XOR (`^`) computes parity.

```verilog
genvar i;
generate
    for (i = 0; i < N; i = i + 1) begin : stage
        adder u_add(.a(a[i]), .b(b[i]), .sum(s[i]));
    end
endgenerate
```
**generate** creates parameterized, replicated hardware structures. Supports `for`, `if`, and `case`.

```verilog
initial begin
    $monitor("Time=%0t clk=%b rst=%b count=%d",
             $time, clk, rst, count);
end
```
**$monitor** prints whenever any listed signal changes. Only one active monitor at a time. Use `$monitoroff`/`$monitoron`.

```verilog
initial begin
    #1000 $finish;
end

initial begin
    if (error_count > 10) $stop;  // pause simulation
end
```
**$finish** ends simulation. **$stop** pauses and enters interactive mode. Both used in testbenches.

```verilog
$time      // returns current simulation time (64-bit)
$stime     // 32-bit time
$realtime  // real-valued time
```
**$time** returns the current simulation time. Used with `$display` and `$monitor` for debugging.

```verilog
initial begin
    $dumpfile("wave.vcd");
    $dumpvars(0, testbench);  // dump all signals
    $dumpvars(1, uut);       // depth=1
end
```
**$dumpvars** generates VCD waveform output. Depth 0 = all hierarchy. View with GTKWave or similar.

```verilog
`define DATA_WIDTH 32
`define MAX(a,b) ((a)>(b)?(a):(b))

wire [`DATA_WIDTH-1:0] bus;
assign result = `MAX(x, y);
```
**`define** creates text macros. Referenced with backtick. Supports parameterized macros.

```verilog
`include "defines.vh"
`include "interfaces.sv"
```
**`include** inserts the contents of another file. Use for shared definitions, parameters, and interfaces.

```verilog
assign bus = {a, b, c};           // concatenation
assign word = {4{nibble}};        // replication: 4 copies
assign result = {sign, {7{1'b0}}, mantissa};
```
**Concatenation** `{}` joins signals. **Replication** `{n{expr}}` repeats n times.

```verilog
8'b1010_0011   // 8-bit binary
8'hA3          // 8-bit hex
8'd163         // 8-bit decimal
16'o377        // 16-bit octal
4'bx           // 4-bit unknown
4'bz           // 4-bit high-impedance
```
**Number literals** format: `<size>'<base><value>`. Underscore `_` improves readability.

```verilog
always @(posedge clk)   // rising edge
always @(negedge clk)   // falling edge
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)  // async reset
```
**posedge/negedge** detect signal transitions. Used in sensitivity lists for edge-triggered behavior.

```verilog
always @(a or b or sel)   // explicit list
always @(*)               // all inputs (Verilog-2001)
always_comb               // SystemVerilog combinational
always_ff @(posedge clk)  // SystemVerilog sequential
```
**Sensitivity list** controls when `always` blocks execute. `@(*)` automatically includes all read signals.

```pla
PROGRAM program_name;
```
Declares the beginning of a PL/A program unit, establishing the program name and scope. The program block contains all declarations, procedures, and executable statements for the application.

```pla
PROCEDURE name(params) -> return_type;
```
Declares a named procedure in PL/A that encapsulates a sequence of statements and optionally accepts parameters. Procedures support recursion and can be nested within other program blocks.

```pla
FUNCTION name(params): return_type;
```
Declares a named function in PL/A that computes and returns a value of the specified type. Functions can be used in expressions and support parameter passing by value or reference.

```pla
RECORD field1: type1; field2: type2; END;
```
Defines a composite data structure in PL/A containing named fields of potentially different types, allowing structured data grouping for complex state representation in programs.

```pla
STRING[maxlength]
```
The character string type in PL/A representing a sequence of characters with a declared maximum length. Supports concatenation, substring extraction, and comparison operations.

```pla
INTEGER
```
The signed whole number type in PL/A supporting standard arithmetic operations including addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulo for numeric computing tasks.

```pla
REAL
```
The floating-point numeric type in PL/A for representing decimal numbers with fractional components. Used for scientific computations and financial calculations requiring non-integer precision.

```pla
BOOLEAN
```
The logical data type in PL/A with exactly two values TRUE and FALSE, used for conditional expressions, control flow decisions, and predicate evaluations in program logic.

```pla
ARRAY[lower..upper] OF element_type
```
A fixed-size indexed collection of elements of uniform type in PL/A, declared with explicit lower and upper bounds. Supports element access by subscript and can be multidimensional.

```pla
CONST name = value;
```
Declares a named constant in PL/A whose value is fixed at compile time and cannot be modified during program execution. Constants improve readability and prevent accidental value changes.

```pla
VAR name: type;
```
Declares a variable in PL/A with a specified type, allocating storage for runtime data. Variables must be declared before use and can be initialized at the point of declaration.

```pla
READ(var1, var2, ...)
```
Reads input values from the standard input stream and assigns them to the specified variables in order. Performs implicit type conversion based on the variable types declared.

```pla
WRITE(expr1, expr2, ...)
```
Outputs the values of the specified expressions to the standard output stream. Supports formatted output with field width specifiers for aligned tabular display of data.

```pla
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```pla
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```pla
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```pla
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```pla
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```pla
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```pla
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```pla
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```pla
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
